rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/4P3/8/PPPP1PPP/RNB.text: Stakes a claim in the center and frees the king's bishop and queen; the most direct fight for central space.

rnbqkbnr/pppp1ppp/8/4p3/4P3/8/PPPP1PPP/R.text: Black answers symmetrically, contesting the center and refusing to cede space.

rnbqkbnr/pppp1ppp/8/4p3/4P3/5N2/PPPP1PPP.text: Develops a piece while attacking e5, forcing Black to react and gaining time.

r1bqkbnr/pppp1ppp/2n5/4p3/4P3/5N2/PPPP1P.text: Defends e5 and develops toward the center; the most natural reply.

r1bqkbnr/pppp1ppp/2n5/4p3/2B1P3/5N2/PPPP.text: Aims the bishop at the f7 square, Black's weakest point, while preparing to castle.

r1bqk1nr/pppp1ppp/2n5/2b1p3/2B1P3/5N2/PP.text: Mirrors White, targeting f2 and contesting the a7-g1 diagonal; the Giuoco Piano proper.

r1bqk1nr/pppp1ppp/2n5/2b1p3/2B1P3/2P2N2/.text: Prepares d4 to build a broad pawn center and opens a retreat square for the bishop on c2.

r1bqk2r/pppp1ppp/2n2n2/2b1p3/2B1P3/2P2N2.text: Develops with tempo by eyeing e4, and prepares to castle into safety.

r1bqk2r/pppp1ppp/2n2n2/2b1p3/2B1P3/2PP1N.text: The quiet, modern Giuoco Pianissimo: White supports e4 and plans slow maneuvering rather than an early d4 break.

r1bqk2r/ppp2ppp/2np1n2/2b1p3/2B1P3/2PP1N.text: Black mirrors, solidifying e5 and opening the path for the light-squared bishop.

r1bqkb1r/pppp1ppp/2n2n2/4p3/2B1P3/5N2/PP.text: The Two Knights Defense: Black counterattacks e4 immediately instead of defending with the bishop.

r1bqkb1r/pppp1ppp/2n2n2/4p3/2BPP3/5N2/PP.text: Strikes in the center at once, opening lines before Black is fully developed.

r1bqkb1r/pppp1ppp/2n2n2/8/2BpP3/5N2/PPP2.text: Accepts the pawn to win central material, accepting a lead in White's development as compensation.

r1bqkbnr/pppp1ppp/2n5/1B2p3/4P3/5N2/PPPP.text: The Ruy Lopez: the bishop pins the knight that defends e5, applying long-term pressure on Black's center.

r1bqkbnr/1ppp1ppp/p1n5/1B2p3/4P3/5N2/PPP.text: The Morphy Defense: questions the bishop immediately, forcing it to commit before Black continues.

r1bqkbnr/1ppp1ppp/p1n5/4p3/B3P3/5N2/PPPP.text: Retreats but keeps the pin alive, maintaining pressure on c6 along the diagonal.

r1bqkb1r/1ppp1ppp/p1n2n2/4p3/B3P3/5N2/PP.text: Counterattacks e4 and develops, hurrying toward castling.
Tucks the king to safety and connects the rooks; White offers the e4 pawn as a temporary gambit relying on quick play.

r1bqk2r/1pppbppp/p1n2n2/4p3/B3P3/5N2/PPP.text: A solid developing move preparing to castle, declining the sharp open lines of other defenses.
Defends e4 with the rook so White can develop the queenside without dropping the pawn.

r1bqk2r/2ppbppp/p1n2n2/1p2p3/B3P3/5N2/PP.text: Gains queenside space and forces the bishop back, freeing Black's position.

r1bqk2r/2ppbppp/p1n2n2/1p2p3/4P3/1B3N2/P.text: Retreats to a strong diagonal still bearing down on f7 and the center.

r1bqk2r/2p1bppp/p1np1n2/1p2p3/4P3/1B3N2/.text: Solidifies e5 and opens the path for the light-squared bishop, entering the Closed Ruy main lines.

r1bqkb1r/pppp1ppp/2n2n2/1B2p3/4P3/5N2/PP.text: The Berlin Defense: Black ignores the pin and counterattacks e4, heading for a solid, durable structure.
Castles and offers e4, trusting development over pawn-grabbing.

r1bqkb1r/pppp1ppp/2n5/1B2p3/4n3/5N2/PPPP.text: Snaps off the pawn; the famous Berlin tabiya where Black trades into a resilient endgame.

r1bqkb1r/pppp1ppp/2n5/1B2p3/3Pn3/5N2/PPP.text: Opens the center to exploit Black's slightly exposed pieces and regain the initiative.

r1bqkb1r/pppp1ppp/2nn4/1B2p3/3P4/5N2/PPP.text: Retreats the knight to hit the bishop and untangle, steering toward the Berlin endgame.

rnbqkbnr/pp1ppppp/8/2p5/4P3/8/PPPP1PPP/R.text: The Sicilian: Black declines symmetry and fights for d4 from the flank, aiming for an unbalanced, counterattacking game.

rnbqkbnr/pp1ppppp/8/2p5/4P3/5N2/PPPP1PPP.text: Develops and prepares the central break d4.

rnbqkbnr/pp2pppp/3p4/2p5/4P3/5N2/PPPP1PP.text: Supports a future ...e5 and opens the light bishop's diagonal; a flexible Najdorf/Scheveningen setup.

rnbqkbnr/pp2pppp/3p4/2p5/3PP3/5N2/PPP2PP.text: Strikes in the center, trading the c-pawn for a half-open d-file and free piece play.

rnbqkbnr/pp2pppp/3p4/8/3pP3/5N2/PPP2PPP/.text: Captures, giving Black the half-open c-file for counterplay against c2 and the queenside.

rnbqkbnr/pp2pppp/3p4/8/3NP3/8/PPP2PPP/RN.text: Recaptures with the knight, centralizing it on a strong square.

rnbqkb1r/pp2pppp/3p1n2/8/3NP3/8/PPP2PPP/.text: Develops and attacks e4, forcing White to defend the pawn.

rnbqkb1r/pp2pppp/3p1n2/8/3NP3/2N5/PPP2PP.text: Defends e4 and develops, reaching the main Open Sicilian structure.

rnbqkb1r/1p2pppp/p2p1n2/8/3NP3/2N5/PPP2P.text: The Najdorf move: controls b5, denying White's pieces that square and preparing ...e5 or ...b5 expansion without allowing a knight or bishop to land on b5.

rnbqkb1r/pp2pp1p/3p1np1/8/3NP3/2N5/PPP2P.text: The Dragon: Black fianchettoes to put the bishop on the long diagonal, aiming it at the center and White's queenside.

rnbqkb1r/pp2pp1p/3p1np1/8/3NP3/2N1B3/PPP.text: Develops and prepares queenside castling with a kingside pawn storm to follow.

rnbqk2r/pp2ppbp/3p1np1/8/3NP3/2N1B3/PPP2.text: Completes the fianchetto; the bishop becomes a powerful long-range attacker.

rnbqkbnr/pppp1ppp/4p3/8/4P3/8/PPPP1PPP/R.text: The French: Black prepares ...d5 to challenge e4 while keeping a solid, if cramped, structure.

rnbqkbnr/pppp1ppp/4p3/8/3PP3/8/PPP2PPP/R.text: Builds the big center, daring Black to strike at it.

rnbqkbnr/ppp2ppp/4p3/3p4/3PP3/8/PPP2PPP/.text: Challenges e4 directly; the defining French tension.

rnbqkbnr/ppp2ppp/4p3/3p4/3PP3/2N5/PPP2PP.text: Defends e4 and develops, keeping the central tension.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/4pn2/3p4/3PP3/2N5/PPP2P.text: Pressures e4 again, forcing White to resolve the center.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/4pn2/3pP3/3P4/2N5/PPP2P.text: Advances and gains space, locking the center and gaining a kingside cramp at the cost of a fixed target on e5/d4.

rnbqkb1r/pppn1ppp/4p3/3pP3/3P4/2N5/PPP2P.text: Retreats to prepare the freeing ...c5 break against White's pawn chain base on d4.

rnbqkb1r/pppn1ppp/4p3/3pP3/3P1P2/2N5/PPP.text: Buttresses e5 and gains kingside space, supporting White's space advantage.

rnbqkb1r/pp1n1ppp/4p3/2ppP3/3P1P2/2N5/PP.text: Black attacks the base of the pawn chain on d4, the thematic French counterstrike.

rnbqkbnr/pp1ppppp/2p5/8/4P3/8/PPPP1PPP/R.text: The Caro-Kann: prepares ...d5 to hit e4 while keeping the light bishop's diagonal open, unlike the French.

rnbqkbnr/pp2pppp/2p5/3p4/3PP3/8/PPP2PPP/.text: Challenges e4 with solid support behind it.

rnbqkbnr/pp2pppp/2p5/3p4/3PP3/2N5/PPP2PP.text: Develops and defends e4, inviting the classical lines.

rnbqkbnr/pp2pppp/2p5/8/3Pp3/2N5/PPP2PPP/.text: Resolves the tension, handing White the center but gaining easy development and a sound structure.

rn1qkbnr/pp2pppp/2p5/5b2/3PN3/8/PPP2PPP/.text: Develops the light bishop actively outside the pawn chain before playing ...e6, a key Caro-Kann advantage over the French.

rn1qkbnr/pp2pppp/2p5/5b2/3P4/6N1/PPP2PPP.text: Attacks the bishop, forcing it to declare its square.

rn1qkbnr/pp2pppp/2p3b1/8/3P4/6N1/PPP2PPP.text: Retreats to safety while keeping the bishop on its strong diagonal.

rnbqkbnr/ppp1pppp/8/3p4/4P3/8/PPPP1PPP/R.text: The Scandinavian: Black immediately challenges e4, forcing a resolution before White develops.

rnbqkbnr/ppp1pppp/8/3P4/8/8/PPPP1PPP/RNB.text: Accepts the central pawn, the principled try.

rnb1kbnr/ppp1pppp/8/3q4/8/8/PPPP1PPP/RNB.text: Recaptures immediately, regaining material but exposing the queen to tempo gains.

rnb1kbnr/ppp1pppp/8/3q4/8/2N5/PPPP1PPP/R.text: Develops with tempo by attacking the queen, the point of White's setup.

rnb1kbnr/ppp1pppp/8/q7/8/2N5/PPPP1PPP/R1.text: Retreats to an active square, pinning nothing but eyeing the queenside and staying safe.

rnb1kbnr/ppp1pppp/8/q7/3P4/2N5/PPP2PPP/R.text: Seizes the full center while the queen sits offside.

rnb1kb1r/ppp1pppp/5n2/q7/3P4/2N5/PPP2PPP.text: Develops and prepares ...c6/...Bf5 with a solid, slightly passive structure.

rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/3P4/8/PPP1PPPP/RNB.text: Claims the center on the queenside and opens lines for the dark bishop and queen.

rnbqkbnr/ppp1pppp/8/3p4/2PP4/8/PP2PPPP/R.text: The Queen's Gambit: offers the c-pawn to deflect Black's d5 and dominate the center.

rnbqkbnr/ppp2ppp/4p3/3p4/2PP4/8/PP2PPPP/.text: Declines the gambit, solidifying d5 — at the cost of temporarily blocking in the c8 bishop.

rnbqkbnr/ppp2ppp/4p3/3p4/2PP4/2N5/PP2PPP.text: Develops and adds pressure to d5.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/4pn2/3p4/2PP4/2N5/PP2PP.text: Develops and defends d5 against the mounting pressure.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/4pn2/3p2B1/2PP4/2N5/PP2.text: Pins the f6 knight, increasing pressure on d5 by undermining its defender.

rnbqk2r/ppp1bppp/4pn2/3p2B1/2PP4/2N5/PP2.text: Breaks the pin's sting and prepares to castle, a solid classical setup.

rnbqk2r/ppp1bppp/4pn2/3p2B1/2PP4/2N1P3/P.text: Opens the f1 bishop's path and supports d4, a flexible, sound build.

rnbq1rk1/ppp1bppp/4pn2/3p2B1/2PP4/2N1P3/.text: Black castles into safety, completing kingside development.

rnbqkbnr/ppp1pppp/8/8/2pP4/8/PP2PPPP/RNB.text: Accepts the pawn, conceding the center temporarily to develop freely and target the c-file later.

rnbqkbnr/ppp1pppp/8/8/2pP4/5N2/PP2PPPP/R.text: Develops and controls e5, discouraging ...e5 before White regains the pawn.

rnbqkb1r/ppp1pppp/5n2/8/2pP4/5N2/PP2PPPP.text: Develops and eyes the center without rushing to defend the extra c4 pawn, which is hard to hold.

rnbqkb1r/ppp1pppp/5n2/8/2pP4/4PN2/PP3PPP.text: Opens the bishop's path to recapture on c4 and supports the center.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/4pn2/8/2pP4/4PN2/PP3PPP.text: Frees the king's bishop and prepares ...c5 to challenge d4.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/4pn2/8/2BP4/4PN2/PP3PPP.text: Regains the gambit pawn, the bishop landing on an active diagonal toward f7.

rnbqkbnr/pp2pppp/2p5/3p4/2PP4/8/PP2PPPP/.text: The Slav: supports d5 with a pawn while keeping the c8 bishop's diagonal open, solving the QGD's bad-bishop problem.

rnbqkb1r/pp2pppp/2p2n2/8/2pP4/2N2N2/PP2P.text: Grabs the pawn, planning ...b5 to hold it or ...Bf5 with free development — the open-diagonal point of the Slav.

rnbqkb1r/pp2pppp/2p2n2/8/P1pP4/2N2N2/1P2.text: Stops ...b5, fighting to recover the c4 pawn at the cost of weakening b4.

rn1qkb1r/pp2pppp/2p2n2/5b2/P1pP4/2N2N2/1.text: Develops the bishop actively outside the chain before ...e6, the Slav's key asset.

rnbqkbnr/ppp1pppp/8/3p4/3P4/5N2/PPP1PPPP.text: Develops and controls e5 without committing the c-pawn.

rnbqkb1r/ppp1pppp/5n2/3p4/3P1B2/5N2/PPP1.text: The London System: develops the bishop outside the pawn chain to a strong square before playing e3, avoiding the QGD's bad bishop.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/4pn2/3p4/3P1B2/5N2/PPP1.text: Solid setup freeing the king's bishop and preparing to castle.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/4pn2/3p4/3P1B2/4PN2/PPP.text: Solidifies d4, opens the f1 bishop, and completes the London pawn triangle.

rnbqk2r/ppp2ppp/3bpn2/3p4/3P1B2/4PN2/PPP.text: Offers to trade the strong London bishop or challenge the f4 square.

rnbqk2r/ppp2ppp/3bpn2/3p4/3P4/4PNB1/PPP2.text: Sidesteps the trade, keeping the bishop on its active diagonal.

rnbqkb1r/pppppppp/5n2/8/3P4/8/PPP1PPPP/R.text: Controls e4 and develops, declining to commit pawns in the center yet — a hypermodern approach.

rnbqkb1r/pppppppp/5n2/8/2PP4/8/PP2PPPP/R.text: Grabs more central space, the Queen's Gambit family setup.

rnbqkb1r/pppppp1p/5np1/8/2PP4/8/PP2PPPP/.text: Prepares the fianchetto, the King's Indian signature: Black cedes the center to attack it later with pieces and pawn breaks.

rnbqk2r/ppppppbp/5np1/8/2PP4/2N5/PP2PPPP.text: Completes the fianchetto, the bishop bearing down the long diagonal toward d4.

rnbqk2r/ppppppbp/5np1/8/2PPP3/2N5/PP3PPP.text: Builds the full broad center, accepting the challenge to defend it against ...e5 and ...c5 breaks.

rnbqk2r/ppp1ppbp/3p1np1/8/2PPP3/2N5/PP3P.text: Supports a future ...e5 and opens the light bishop, the classical KID setup.

rnbqk2r/ppp1ppbp/3p1np1/8/2PPP3/2N2N2/PP.text: Develops and guards e5/d4 before castling.

rnbq1rk1/ppp1ppbp/3p1np1/8/2PPP3/2N2N2/P.text: Black castles, ready to strike the center with ...e5.

rnbqkb1r/pppp1ppp/4pn2/8/2PP4/8/PP2PPPP/.text: Frees the king's bishop and prepares either ...d5 or ...Bb4.

rnbqkb1r/pppp1ppp/4pn2/8/2PP4/2N5/PP2PPP.text: Develops and fights for e4 and d5.

rnbqk2r/pppp1ppp/4pn2/8/1bPP4/2N5/PP2PPP.text: The Nimzo-Indian: pins the knight defending e4, fighting for the center with pieces and threatening to double White's pawns via ...Bxc3.

rnbqk2r/pppp1ppp/4pn2/8/1bPP4/2N1P3/PP3P.text: The Rubinstein: a flexible, solid move opening the bishop and supporting d4 while awaiting Black's plan.

rnbq1rk1/pppp1ppp/4pn2/8/1bPP4/2N1P3/PP3.text: Black castles, completing kingside safety before resolving the central tension.

rnbqkb1r/pppp1ppp/4pn2/8/2PP4/5N2/PP2PPP.text: Develops and controls e5, sidestepping the Nimzo pin.

rnbqkb1r/p1pp1ppp/1p2pn2/8/2PP4/5N2/PP2P.text: The Queen's Indian: prepares to fianchetto the light bishop to contest e4 along the long diagonal, since White avoided Nc3.

rnbqkb1r/p1pp1ppp/1p2pn2/8/2PP4/5NP1/PP2.text: Prepares White's own fianchetto to neutralize the long diagonal and fight for the light squares.

rn1qkb1r/pbpp1ppp/1p2pn2/8/2PP4/5NP1/PP2.text: Completes the fianchetto, the bishop pressuring e4 from afar.
Contests the long diagonal and prepares to castle, a harmonious setup.

rn1qk2r/pbppbppp/1p2pn2/8/2PP4/5NP1/PP2P.text: A flexible developing move preparing to castle.

rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/2P5/8/PP1PPPPP/RNB.text: The English: stakes out d5 from the flank, a flexible reversed-Sicilian approach that delays central commitment.

rnbqkbnr/pppp1ppp/8/4p3/2P5/8/PP1PPPPP/R.text: Black seizes the center, mirroring as if playing a Sicilian a tempo up.

rnbqkbnr/pppp1ppp/8/4p3/2P5/2N5/PP1PPPPP.text: Develops and fights for d5, the key contested square.

rnbqkb1r/pppp1ppp/5n2/4p3/2P5/2N5/PP1PPP.text: Develops and contests e4/d5 in turn.

rnbqkb1r/pppp1ppp/5n2/4p3/2P5/2N2N2/PP1P.text: Develops and attacks e5, gaining a tempo.

r1bqkb1r/pppp1ppp/2n2n2/4p3/2P5/2N2NP1/P.text: Prepares the fianchetto to pressure d5 and the long light diagonal, the English's thematic plan.

r1bqkb1r/ppp2ppp/2n2n2/3pp3/2P5/2N2NP1/P.text: Black strikes back in the center, gaining space and challenging White's setup.

rnbqkb1r/pppp1ppp/4pn2/8/2PP4/6P1/PP2PP1.text: The Catalan: prepares the fianchetto to exert long-range pressure on the queenside light squares and the c4/d5 complex.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/4pn2/3p4/2PP4/6P1/PP2PP.text: Black challenges the center, leaning on the c4 pawn.
Completes the fianchetto, the bishop becoming a long-term asset bearing on d5 and the queenside.

rnbqk2r/ppp1bppp/4pn2/3p4/2PP4/6P1/PP2PP.text: A solid developing move preparing to castle into the Closed Catalan.

rnbqk2r/ppp1bppp/4pn2/3p4/2PP4/5NP1/PP2P.text: Develops and controls e5 before castling.

rnbq1rk1/ppp1bppp/4pn2/3p4/2PP4/5NP1/PP2.text: Black castles, ready to resolve the central tension with ...dxc4 or ...c6.

rnbqkbnr/ppp1pppp/3p4/8/4P3/8/PPPP1PPP/R.text: The Pirc/Modern setup: Black prepares ...Nf6 and ...g6, ceding the center to attack it later, hypermodern style.

rnbqkbnr/ppp1pppp/3p4/8/3PP3/8/PPP2PPP/R.text: Builds the broad center White is invited to overextend.

rnbqkb1r/ppp1pppp/3p1n2/8/3PP3/8/PPP2PPP.text: Attacks e4, forcing White to defend and develop.

rnbqkb1r/ppp1pppp/3p1n2/8/3PP3/2N5/PPP2P.text: Defends e4 and develops toward the center.

rnbqkb1r/ppp1pp1p/3p1np1/8/3PP3/2N5/PPP2.text: Prepares the fianchetto, aiming the bishop at White's center along the long diagonal.

rnbqkb1r/ppp1pp1p/3p1np1/8/3PP3/2N2N2/PP.text: Develops and guards e5/d4, a solid Classical setup.

rnbqk2r/ppp1ppbp/3p1np1/8/3PP3/2N2N2/PPP.text: Completes the fianchetto, pressuring d4 and the center.

rnbqkbnr/pppppp1p/6p1/8/4P3/8/PPPP1PPP/R.text: The Modern: an immediate fianchetto, ceding the center to counterattack it with pieces and timely pawn breaks.

rnbqkbnr/pppppp1p/6p1/8/3PP3/8/PPP2PPP/R.text: Occupies the full center, accepting the invitation to expand.

rnbqk1nr/ppppppbp/6p1/8/3PP3/8/PPP2PPP/R.text: Develops the bishop to the long diagonal, eyeing d4 and the center.

rnbqk1nr/ppp1ppbp/3p2p1/8/3PP3/2N5/PPP2P.text: Supports a later ...e5 and opens the light bishop, a flexible Modern structure.

r1bqkbnr/pppp1ppp/2n5/4p3/3PP3/5N2/PPP2P.text: The Scotch: strikes the center at once, opening lines before Black completes development.

r1bqkbnr/pppp1ppp/2n5/8/3pP3/5N2/PPP2PPP.text: Captures to win central space; declining leaves White a strong center.

r1bqkbnr/pppp1ppp/2n5/8/3NP3/8/PPP2PPP/R.text: Recaptures, centralizing the knight on a dominant square.

r1bqk1nr/pppp1ppp/2n5/2b5/3NP3/8/PPP2PPP.text: Develops with pressure on d4, attacking the centralized knight.

rnbqkbnr/pppp1ppp/8/4p3/4P3/2N5/PPPP1PPP.text: The Vienna: develops the knight and supports a later f4 break, a flexible alternative to Nf3.

rnbqkb1r/pppp1ppp/5n2/4p3/4P3/2N5/PPPP1P.text: Develops and attacks e4, the most testing reply.

rnbqkb1r/pppp1ppp/5n2/4p3/4PP2/2N5/PPPP2.text: The Vienna Gambit: strikes at e5 to open lines and seize the initiative on the kingside.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/5n2/3pp3/4PP2/2N5/PPPP2.text: Black counters in the center, the principled response that frees his game.

rnbqkb1r/pppp1ppp/5n2/4p3/4P3/5N2/PPPP1P.text: The Petroff: instead of defending e5, Black counterattacks e4, aiming for symmetry and solidity.

rnbqkb1r/pppp1ppp/5n2/4N3/4P3/8/PPPP1PPP.text: Grabs the pawn; capturing back immediately would walk into a tempo-losing trap.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/3p1n2/4N3/4P3/8/PPPP1PP.text: Dislodges the knight before recapturing, the accurate move order.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/3p1n2/8/4P3/5N2/PPPP1PP.text: Retreats to safety, controlling the center.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/3p4/8/4n3/5N2/PPPP1PPP/.text: Recaptures, restoring material balance in the symmetrical Petroff structure.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/3p4/8/3Pn3/5N2/PPP2PPP/.text: Opens the center and gains space, the main try for an edge.

rnbqkb1r/ppp2ppp/8/3p4/3Pn3/5N2/PPP2PPP/.text: Supports the e4 knight and stakes a central claim, equalizing space.

rnbqkbnr/ppppp1pp/8/5p2/3P4/8/PPP1PPPP/R.text: The Dutch: Black grabs kingside space and control of e4, fighting for the initiative at the cost of slight king weakening.

rnbqkbnr/ppppp1pp/8/5p2/3P4/6P1/PPP1PP1P.text: Prepares the fianchetto to contest the long light diagonal Black has weakened, the main antidote.

rnbqkb1r/ppppp1pp/5n2/5p2/3P4/6P1/PPP1PP.text: Develops and reinforces control of e4.
Completes the fianchetto, pressuring the center and queenside.

rnbqkb1r/pppp2pp/4pn2/5p2/3P4/6P1/PPP1PP.text: Frees the king's bishop and prepares a Classical Dutch setup with ...Be7 and ...d6.
